title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: Tel Aviv, Israel / New York (Global, distributed)
description: Finout, a cloud cost-management B2B SaaS backed by $85M+ and trusted by brands like The New York Times and Wiz, seeks a hands-on Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO. The role builds and scales the operational backbone of the GTM engine. Requires 7+ years in RevOps/Sales Ops/Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue-analytics expertise, and cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR, collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Exceptional match: 15+ years directly exceeds 7+ requirement, B2B SaaS fintech expertise aligns perfectly with Finout's domain, senior RevOps background matches Head of Revenue Operations level, and experience scaling high-growth companies demonstrates capability to build operational backbone. Tel Aviv location is ideal fit.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's expansion and your Head of Revenue Operations opening caught my attention—your mission to operationalize GTM for high-growth B2B SaaS companies like Finout resonates deeply. With 15+ years scaling RevOps across B2B SaaS and fintech, including experience building revenue operations infrastructure at companies scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, I've developed the exact Salesforce, analytics, and cross-functional leadership expertise you're looking for. I'd welcome a conversation about how I can build Finout's operational engine to accelerate your next growth phase.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, a leader in revenue intelligence, seeks a RevOps Manager focused on territory strategy and operations to accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy and scalable territory design. Responsibilities include territory mapping in Fullcast/Salesforce, market expansion and rep capacity modeling, and cross-functional alignment. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Strategy, or GTM planning, deep understanding of B2B SaaS GTM models, strong analytical skills (Excel, SQL, BI tools like Tableau/Retool), and Salesforce proficiency.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, B2B SaaS GTM depth, and Salesforce proficiency. Territory strategy, pipeline forecasting, and data-driven operations are core strengths, though the role is more specialized in territory design than broader revenue operations leadership.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building RevOps and GTM functions in B2B SaaS, and Gong's territory strategy mandate is exactly the kind of high-leverage problem I thrive on. With deep experience in Salesforce architecture, capacity modeling, and data-driven seller productivity initiatives, I'm excited about the opportunity to scale Gong's revenue operations through intelligent territory design and cross-functional alignment.

title: Senior Revenue Operations Manager
location: Remote (US or Canada, Eastern time zone preferred)
description: IntellaQuest (an ASG company) is bringing in its first RevOps professional to serve as the strategic backbone of its GTM engine. This foundational player-coach role, reporting to the C-suite, builds RevOps from the ground up—architecting systems, processes, and insights driving predictable revenue across Sales, Marketing, and CS. Ideal for 0-to-1 operators. Requires GTM/RevOps experience in Mid-Market/Lower Enterprise motions ($25k–$75k ACV), companies at $5M–$30M ARR, and GTM tech stack expertise (HubSpot, Salesforce, Gong). Base $130k–$140k plus bonus.
score_reason: Excellent match: 15+ years B2B SaaS experience, strong RevOps/GTM background, and proven expertise with HubSpot/Salesforce align perfectly with building RevOps 0-to-1 at $5M–$30M ARR. Remote role within acceptable time zones is ideal; only minor concern is whether IntellaQuest's current stage/ACV range matches peak career experience.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I noticed IntellaQuest is bringing in its first RevOps leader—a foundational 0-to-1 build that typically defines a company's revenue predictability for years. With 15+ years scaling GTM operations across HubSpot and Salesforce environments in B2B SaaS, I've architected the exact systems, forecasting models, and Sales-Marketing-CS alignment you're looking to establish. I'd be interested in exploring how to become that strategic backbone for your team.

title: Revenue Operations Manager
description: VulnCheck seeks a Revenue Operations Manager to design and optimize scalable revenue processes across the full customer lifecycle. Responsibilities include building executive dashboards covering pipeline health, conversion, forecasting, bookings, and churn/expansion. Requires 4+ years in RevOps, Sales Operations, or GTM Systems roles within B2B SaaS, hands-on Salesforce administration (object design, workflow automation, reporting) or HubSpot expertise (lifecycle stages, lead scoring, marketing-to-sales handoff), strong knowledge of SaaS metrics (ARR, ACV, NRR, churn), and experience integrating a broader GTM tech stack.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment across all dimensions: 15+ years B2B SaaS experience far exceeds 4+ year requirement, deep Salesforce/HubSpot expertise matches core technical needs, and demonstrated mastery of pipeline forecasting, SaaS metrics (ARR/ACV/NRR/churn), and GTM tech stack integration directly addresses all stated responsibilities.
ai_opener: I noticed VulnCheck is building out revenue operations to scale your GTM engine—exactly the work I've spent 15+ years executing at scale in B2B SaaS. With hands-on Salesforce architecture experience and a track record of designing dashboards that drive accurate forecasting and pipeline visibility, I'd be well-positioned to design the scalable revenue processes you're looking to implement across your customer lifecycle.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Pylon seeks a GTM Engineer to test, implement, and optimize an AI outbound solution from setup through performance tuning. You'll build automated outbound sequences with high deliverability, own email marketing infrastructure (domain health, warming, deliverability monitoring), create data pipelines between marketing tools, CRM, and sales tooling for attribution, and collaborate with RevOps on lead scoring and routing. Requires 3+ years in marketing operations or GTM/growth engineering at B2B SaaS companies, hands-on automated outbound experience, and strong technical skills with APIs and webhooks.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM background and technical skill set. The GTM Engineer role directly matches his expertise in pipeline infrastructure, marketing automation, and data integration between systems—though the role skews slightly more hands-on technical than typical senior RevOps leadership.
ai_opener: Hi Pylon team—I've spent 15+ years building GTM infrastructure at B2B SaaS companies, and your GTM Engineer role caught my attention because optimizing outbound automation and email deliverability at scale is exactly where I've driven the most impact. I've owned the full stack from domain health and warming protocols through attribution pipelines connecting marketing, sales, and RevOps—and I'm particularly interested in how you're leveraging AI to improve sequence performance and lead scoring accuracy.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Gradient Labs seeks a GTM Engineer for a foundational role owning the systems and signals behind how the company identifies, segments, and reaches its ICP—stitching BDR, marketing, and sales ops into one streamlined operation. You'll build qualified target lists, own end-to-end low-volume, high-quality outbound (email and LinkedIn), and manage deliverability. Requires 5+ years in RevOps, marketing ops, or sales ops at an enterprise SaaS company, account-based motion experience, systems-building instinct, fluency in HubSpot, Clay, and Apollo, and genuine AI fluency.
score_reason: Excellent alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM background, systems-building expertise, and AI-native proficiency. Role matches his target positions perfectly, though 5+ years requirement is slightly below his 15+ year seniority level and low-volume outbound is narrower than typical scope.
ai_opener: Omri—Gradient Labs' vision of stitching BDR, marketing, and sales ops into one AI-driven system resonates deeply with modern RevOps. Your GTM Engineer role is exactly the kind of foundational systems-building work that separates high-performing go-to-market engines from the rest, and your emphasis on ICP precision and deliverability suggests you're optimizing for quality over noise—something we see as the GTM differentiation of 2025.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Supabase, the Postgres development platform built by developers for developers, seeks a GTM Engineer to join its Growth and RevOps teams and make go-to-market teams dramatically more productive. Working closely with Growth, RevOps, Sales, and Success, you'll translate product usage and customer signals into actionable workflows—improving how opportunities are identified, prioritized, and converted across the funnel. This is a systems and execution role focused on increasing the efficiency of the PLG-to-revenue engine, ideal for someone who thrives in async, fast-paced environments.
score_reason: Perfect alignment with GTM Engineer role targeting RevOps/Growth systems work in B2B SaaS. Remote location, PLG-to-revenue engine focus, and Postgres/developer-first product match Omri's expertise in pipeline efficiency and operational execution.
ai_opener: Supabase's approach to building a PLG-to-revenue engine resonates deeply—I've spent 15+ years architecting exactly these systems at scale in B2B SaaS. Your need for someone who can translate product signals into actionable RevOps workflows is precisely where I've driven the most impact: designing pipeline forecasting models, optimizing funnel efficiency, and building the systems that let GTM teams operate at velocity.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo, an AI research platform enabling fast, affordable, high-quality consumer and B2B research used by global brands like Unilever, Google, and Orange, seeks a GTM Engineer. As the company scales toward 20x revenue in 2026, you'll replace manual processes and spreadsheets with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows—think learning lead-scoring models, working attribution pipelines, faster onboarding flows, and AI agents eliminating hours of manual work. Requires someone who can code, ship, and iterate on internal tools and automations that make the GTM team highly effective.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM Engineer title, RevOps automation, and AI-native workflows (lead scoring, attribution, onboarding). Weak fit on the coding/shipping requirement—Omri is a RevOps strategist, not a software engineer, which is the core ask here.
ai_opener: Conveo's vision of replacing spreadsheets with AI-powered GTM workflows resonates deeply—I've spent 15 years automating exactly these processes (lead scoring, attribution, pipeline forecasting). However, I want to be transparent: my strength is designing and orchestrating these systems via Salesforce/HubSpot automation and operational strategy, not writing production code. If you need a GTM leader to architect what engineers build, I'm your person. If you need someone shipping Python and deploying models, I'd recommend pairing me with an actual engineer.

title: Head of Growth Marketing
description: Flex seeks a Head of Growth Marketing to lead growth, digital, and performance marketing efforts. Requires 6–10+ years of experience in growth, digital, or performance marketing—ideally FinTech or B2B SaaS—plus 3+ years managing and mentoring high-performing teams. Candidates should have proven success scaling organizations from $50M to $200M+ ARR, scaling self-serve acquisition, activation, and monetization, plus deep expertise in SEO, paid acquisition, website optimization, and demand generation. Fluency in intent-based targeting, personalization, trigger-based messaging, and predictive analytics, with strong storytelling skills.
score_reason: Strong alignment on B2B SaaS, FinTech, pipeline scaling (ARR growth), and team leadership experience. However, this role emphasizes performance/digital marketing expertise (paid acquisition, SEO, demand gen) rather than RevOps infrastructure, systems, and forecasting where Omri's core strength lies—it's a marketing leadership role, not a revenue operations role.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed Flex is scaling growth marketing and you've built systems-driven GTM engines at scale. While this Head of Growth Marketing role focuses on demand gen and paid channels, your RevOps + GTM background could be valuable if they need someone to architect the revenue infrastructure behind those marketing motions—pipeline forecasting, CRM optimization, and cross-functional analytics. Worth a conversation?
